Play Duration: 9 minutes 9m Brought to you by 936 ABC Hobart Presented by Mel Bush Tasmania's east coast is rich with stories of ghosts said to haunt the many historical properties in the area. Treasurer of the Glamorgan Spring Bay Historical Society, Noel Stanley tells Tasmania Saturdays presenter Mel Bush some of the many tales that have become part of East Coast folklore. "You have to be careful because if you put it out there that you've got a ghost, there are people who will want to come and stay and they're disappointed when they don't experience anything and equally you've got people who will run a mile," he says. Credits Mel Bush, Presenter Grace McCallum, Producer Image Details Meredith House on Tasmania's East Coast.(Supplied: Noel Stanley) Program:More from Tasmania Saturdays Hobart, Launceston, History.
title: The 'haunted' historic houses of Tasmania's east coast

Play Duration: 21 minutes 39 seconds21m This weeks spot on the map takes us to Kilcoy which is approximately 100km northwest of Brisbane. This town is known for it's great steaks and horse racing but it's also known for Yowie sightings. James McAulay tells Rod Quinn how Kilcoy became the Yowie capital of Australia on this weeks spot on the map. Image Details The newly installed yowie is the third version of the same statue to stand at yowie park since the 1980s.(ABC Southern Queensland: Nathan.

Play Duration: 6 minutes 24 seconds6m Brought to you by ABC Sunshine Coast Presented by Annie Gaffney Imagine this... you've had a long day of work planting trees in the forest in the Sunshine Coast hinterland, you're driving home with two mates at night. Lightning striking overhead and loud noises filtering through from a party nearby. And in the dim light - you see a giant terrifying shape..... That was the experience of Seamus Fitzgerald and his mate.
title: Sighting of mythical 'Yowie' turns Queensland forest workers into believers
